description: Add a three-dot menu to the BrowserToolsCard header to match PluginCard's layout structure, fixing the enable-all switch alignment issue. Revert the incorrect spacer fix from commit f43d83e3. The menu will show MCP server version info and serve as a future hook for server update notifications.

userStories.title: Add serverVersion to config.getState response
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the config.getState response to include the MCP server version so the side panel can display it and detect when updates are available.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfigStateResult in platform/shared/src/index.ts includes an optional serverVersion: string field
handleConfigGetState in platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts includes serverVersion in the response, reading it from the server's package.json version
Existing unit tests for handleConfigGetState still pass
A new test verifies that the serverVersion field is present in the config.getState response
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Add `serverVersion?: string` to `ConfigStateResult` in platform/shared/src/index.ts (line 298). Make it optional so older servers that don't send it don't break the side panel.

In platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts, `handleConfigGetState` (line 245) already has access to `state`. The server version is available via the package.json — check how the health endpoint at platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts accesses version (it reads from state or imports the package.json version). Look for how `version` is set on the health response and use the same source.

In the `sendToExtension` call at line 273, add `serverVersion` to the result object alongside `plugins`, `failedPlugins`, and `browserTools`.

Add a test in platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.test.ts verifying the serverVersion field is present. Follow the existing test pattern (see the 'includes browserTools in the result' test at line 595).

userStories.title: Revert spacer and add BrowserToolsMenu component
userStories.description: As a user, I want the BrowserToolsCard to have a three-dot menu matching the PluginCard layout so that all card headers are visually consistent and the enable-all switches align.
acceptanceCriteria: The spacer div added in commit f43d83e3 is removed from BrowserToolsCard.tsx
A new BrowserToolsMenu component exists at platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsMenu.tsx
BrowserToolsMenu renders a three-dot button (MoreHorizontal icon) matching PluginMenu's button style (h-6 w-6, hover:bg-muted/50)
BrowserToolsMenu's dropdown shows a single info item displaying 'Server v{version}' (non-interactive, informational) when serverVersion is provided
BrowserToolsMenu accepts className prop and renders the same wrapper structure as PluginMenu (div with onClick/onKeyDown stopPropagation, role=presentation)
BrowserToolsCard passes serverVersion prop through to BrowserToolsMenu
BrowserToolsCard header layout is: Trigger + BrowserToolsMenu + Switch wrapper — structurally identical to PluginCard's Trigger + PluginMenu + Switch wrapper
The enable-all switch in BrowserToolsCard aligns vertically with the enable-all switch in PluginCard
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes

userStories.title: Wire serverVersion through App.tsx to BrowserToolsCard
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel to display the MCP server version in the BrowserToolsCard menu so I can see which server version is running.
acceptanceCriteria: App.tsx stores serverVersion from fetchConfigState response in state
App.tsx passes serverVersion to BrowserToolsCard
App.tsx passes serverVersion to SearchResults (which renders BrowserToolsCard during search)
When connected to a server that returns serverVersion, the BrowserToolsMenu shows 'Server v{version}'
When connected to an older server without serverVersion, the menu shows 'Server unknown'
Storybook stories for SidePanel and BrowserToolsCard are updated to include serverVersion
A Storybook story for BrowserToolsMenu is created
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
npm run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx:

1. Add state: `const [serverVersion, setServerVersion] = useState<string | undefined>(undefined);`
2. In `loadPlugins` callback, after `setBrowserTools(result.browserTools ?? [])`, add: `setServerVersion(result.serverVersion);`
3. On disconnect (line 220-227), add: `setServerVersion(undefined);`
4. Pass `serverVersion` to BrowserToolsCard (around line 343 where `<BrowserToolsCard>` is rendered)
5. Pass `serverVersion` to SearchResults (around line 332 where `<SearchResults>` is rendered) — SearchResults renders BrowserToolsCard internally for browser tool filtering

In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/SearchResults.tsx:
1. Add `serverVersion?: string` to `SearchResultsProps`
2. Pass it through to BrowserToolsCard

Update Storybook stories:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/SidePanel.stories.tsx — add serverVersion to BrowserToolsCard renders
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/SearchResults.stories.tsx — add serverVersion prop
- Create platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsMenu.stories.tsx with stories: Default (with version), NoVersion (without version)

The SearchResults component also renders BrowserToolsCard when `browserTools.length > 0` in the search results view. Check how it currently receives and passes browserTools props and follow the same pattern for serverVersion.
